revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:江浦横穴群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.053333|N|138.896667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
{{Japanese Kofun |名称 = {{ill|Enoura Yokoketsu Group|ja|江浦横穴群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoura Yokoketsu Group|1=E-no-ura Yokoanabo Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55522851}} |image = [[File:Enoura ouketsu.jpg|300px|江浦横穴群]] |imageキャプション = 江浦横穴群 |所属 = |location = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Numazu|en|Numazu|qid=Q241037|lt=Numazu City|1=Numazu}} 江浦 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=220|coord={{coord|35|03|12.0|N|138|53|48.0|E|qq=}}|qq=}} |位置 = |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 03 |coordinates = 12.0 |coordinates = 138 |coordinates = 53 |coordinates = 48.0 |omitted = |type = {{ill|Yokoanabo|ja|横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}} |built = 6〜8世紀 |Burial Facility = |area = |Interred Persons = |burial goods = |Historic Site Designation = {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture-Historic Site1 Historic Site|ja|静岡県史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Prefecture-Historic Site1 Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>[https://www.pref.shizuoka.jp/bunka/bk-180/bunkazai/detail/1043401.html 江浦横穴群]（静岡県サイト）</ref> |Special Notes = |地図2 = {{location map+|Japan Shizuoka|width=220|float=center|caption=|places= {{location map~|Japan Shizuoka|coordinates=35|coordinates=7|coordinates=43|lon_deg=138|lon_min=51|lon_sec=25|position=bottom|mark=横穴墓.svg|marksize=28|map_dot_label='''{{ill|Enoura Yokoketsu Group|ja|江浦横穴群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoura Yokoketsu Group|1=E-no-ura Yokoanabo Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55522851}}''' |qq=}} }} }} '''Enoura Yokoketsu Group''' (江浦横穴群) is a group of horizontal {{ill|cave tomb|lt=Yokoanabos|ja|横穴墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cave tomb|qq=}} located in {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Numazu|en|Numazu|qid=Q241037|lt=Numazu City|1=Numazu}}, in the area known as Enoura. The name “Enoura” is also written as “Enoura” or “Enooura”{{Sfn|角川書店|1988|pages=190-191|qq=}}. It was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Shizuoka Prefecture in March 1977 (Showa 52). == Overview == The horizontal cave tomb group that extends from Enoura in Numazu City, Shizuoka Prefecture is known as the Enoura Yokoketsu Group. The Enoura Yokoketsu Group was excavated from and constructed in the tuff – the bedrock of the coastal region along {{ill|Suruga Bay|en|Suruga Bay|qid=Q1201023|lt=Suruga Bay|1=Suruga Bay}}. These tombs are divided into four districts, with 74 tombs in District A, 2 in District B, 14 in District C, and 2 in District D, making a total of 92 tombs. On a hill about 150 meters to the east of the horizontal tomb group and at an elevation of approximately 100 meters, there is a group of small round tombs ({{ill|Enoura Kofun|ja|江浦古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enoura Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), allowing one to observe tombs constructed under a different burial system. All of these sites are considered to date from the late 7th century to the early 8th century{{Sfn|沼津市教育委員会|2002|pages=468-469|qq=}}. == Excavation History == In 1947 (Showa 22), burial goods were collected during a survey by the local research club of {{ill|Numazu Higashi High School|ja|沼津東高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu Higashi High School|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 静岡県立沼津東高等学校|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 静岡県立沼津東高等学校|1=Shizuoka Kenritsu Numazu Higashi Kōtō Gakkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11663167}} in Shizuoka Prefecture{{Sfn|沼津市教育委員会|2020|pages=7-8|qq=}}. During the 1970s, as development progressed around Numazu City and plans were made for a new rural road in the Enoura area, local archaeologists from the {{ill|Sunzu Archaeological Society|ja|駿豆考古学会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunzu Archaeological Society|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} voiced concerns about sacrificing the horizontal tombs. However, as the exact location of the Enoura Yokoketsu Group was unknown at that time, a distribution survey was planned as a project in 1974 (Showa 49). Due to delays from frequent buried cultural property surveys, the investigation was ultimately carried out from March 1 to April 13, 1975 (Showa 50) over a period of one and a half months. The survey was conducted using plane surveying methods; however, due to the steep terrain, contour surveying and a visual check of the currently open features were used as the basis for the investigation. A comprehensive survey of the entire horizontal tomb group is planned for the future{{Sfn|沼津市教育委員会|1975|page=1|qq=}}. == map_size == The horizontal cave tombs that have been officially Historic Site1 as a historic site by Shizuoka Prefecture are those in District A, numbering 74. === map_size of the Horizontal Tombs === Below are examples of horizontal tombs from Districts A, B, and C{{Sfn|沼津市教育委員会|1975|pages=15-17|qq=}}. ==== District A ==== * A28 – Main axis orientation N56°E; Elevation: 58.220 m; Total length: 0.60 m (Opening: ceiling width 0.50 m); lower half buried. * A45 – Main axis orientation N25°E; Elevation: 50.510 m; Total length: 3.90 m (Opening: ceiling width 0.80 m, floor width 1.50 m, height 1.20 m) (Back wall: ceiling width 0.80 m, floor width 1.80 m, height 1.75 m). ==== District B ==== * B1 – Main axis orientation N0°E; Elevation: 49.540 m; Total length: 2.35 m (Opening: ceiling width 0.90 m, floor width 1.00 m, height 0.86 m) (Back wall: ceiling width 1.23 m, floor width 1.31 m, height 1.00 m). * B2 – Main axis orientation N60°E; Elevation: 41.440 m; Total length: 1.27 m (Opening: ceiling width 0.54 m); lower half buried. ==== District C ==== * C1 – Main axis orientation N30°E; Elevation: 37.430 m; Total length: 0.90 m (Opening: ceiling width 0.65 m, floor width 1.10 m, height 0.82 m) (Back wall: ceiling width 0.52 m, floor width 1.11 m, height 0.40 m). * C9 – Main axis orientation N26°E; Elevation: 48.050 m; Total length: 2.56 m (Opening: ceiling width 0.78 m, floor width 0.78 m, height 0.86 m) (Back wall: ceiling width 0.83 m, floor width 0.93 m, height 0.75 m). ==== District D ==== * Currently transtypeed into an orchard; measurements could not be taken due to land reclamation. === Enoura Kofun === Above the horizontal tomb group, at an elevation of 100–120 m, there are four round tombs. Although portions of the ceiling stone, side walls, and back wall of the stone chamber are exposed and parts of the mound's base can be observed, the mound itself has disappeared and its clear outline cannot be confirmed{{Sfn|沼津市教育委員会|1975|pages=5|qq=}}. == burial goods == From the Enoura Yokoketsu Group, certain pieces of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} – such as covered high jars, flat bottles, covered jars, and long-necked bottles – have been collected as burial goods. In addition, iron sword fragments and human bones are reported to have been detected, although the details remain unclear{{Sfn|沼津市教育委員会|2002|pages=472-473|qq=}}. == Gallery == <gallery> Enoura_kouketsu2.jpg|Enoura Yokoketsu Group, District A (Photographed in March 2018) Enoura_koketsu3.jpg|Enoura Yokoketsu Group, District A (Photographed in March 2018) 江浦横穴群A1.jpg|Enoura Yokoketsu Group, District A (Photographed in March 2018) 江浦横穴群A2.jpg|Enoura Yokoketsu Group, District A (Photographed in March 2018) 江浦横穴群A3.jpg|Enoura Yokoketsu Group, District A (Photographed in March 2018) 江浦横穴群A4.jpg|Enoura Yokoketsu Group, District A (Photographed in March 2018) 江浦横穴群A5.jpg|Enoura Yokoketsu Group, District A (Photographed in March 2018) </gallery> == On-site Intypeation == '''location''' * {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|qid=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Numazu|en|Numazu|qid=Q241037|lt=Numazu City|1=Numazu}}, Enoura Daitō and Higashiyama '''On-site Intypeation''' * 23 minutes from {{ill|Numazu Station|en|Numazu Station|qid=Q870594|lt=Numazu Station|1=Numazu Station}} by bus bound for {{ill|Izu Nagaoka Onsen|ja|伊豆長岡温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Nagaoka Onsen|1=Izu Nagaoka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11380613}} (Izu Hakone Bus) or by bus (Tokai Bus) bound for Kibi; alight at the Enoura Central Community Center and walk for 20 minutes. '''Surroundings''' * {{ill|Ida Matsue Kofun Group|ja|井田松江古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ida Matsue Kofun Group|1=Ita-Sungō Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55522855}} – a historic site Historic Site1 by Shizuoka Prefecture == Footnotes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Further Reading == * {{Citebook|和書 | title = Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten 22: Shizuoka Prefecture| publisher = 角川書店| date = 1988|ref={{Sfnref|角川書店|1988|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Citebook|和書 | title = Numazu City History: Archaeological Materials| publisher = 沼津市教育委員会| date = 2002|ref={{Sfnref|沼津市教育委員会|2002|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Citebook|和書 | title = Mass Kofun and Horizontal Tombs| publisher = {{ill|Shizuoka Archaeological Society|ja|静岡県考古学会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shizuoka Archaeological Society|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}| date = 1981|ref={{Sfnref|静岡県考古学会|1981|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Citebook|和書 | title = Numazu City designation1 Survey Report No.6: Survey Report of the Enoura Yokoketsu Group and Kofun Group| publisher = 沼津市教育委員会| date = 1975|ref={{Sfnref|沼津市教育委員会|1975|qq=}}|qq=}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=|last=沼津市歴史民俗資料館|title=Survey Report of the Enoura Yokoketsu Group and Kofun Group|origdate=1975-03|date=1975-03|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/103015|ncid=BA67944481|series=Numazu City designation1 Survey Reports|volume=6|qq=}} * {{Cite book|和書|first=聡|last=木村|first2=香織|last2=神山|title=Numazu Historic Sites Guidebook|origdate=2020-12-25|date=2020-12-25|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/90857|location={{ill|Numazu|en|Numazu|qid=Q241037|lt=Numazu City|1=Numazu}}御幸町16番１号|doi=10.24484/sitereports.90857|ref={{Sfnref|沼津市教育委員会|2020|qq=}}|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Enoura lateral hole tombs|qq=}} * [https://www.city.numazu.shizuoka.jp/shisei/profile/bunkazai/kofun/enoura.htm] – Numazu City (local government)
== Distribution and Notable Yokoanabo T) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs == * {{ill|E-no-ura Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eura Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Numazu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs === Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Yokoanabos|en|Hōōzan Yokoanabos|qid=Q18339249|lt=Hōōzan Yokoanabos|1=Hōōzan Cave Tombs}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaga, Ishikawa|qq=}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|qid=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs === Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoanabos|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaida Yokoanabos|qq=}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwara, Osaka|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfuku-ji (Kashiwara)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anfukuji Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs === Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūōmen Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsue|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|qid=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsunedani Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikonishi Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo, Shimane|qq=}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuku Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chibu, Shimane|qq=}}, [[:en:Oki District|Oki District]]) === Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenami Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukuhashi|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiroyama Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuchi, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizumachi Site|qq=}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nōgata|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoanabo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kozuki Yokoanabo|qq=}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Nagino Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Tamana|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamana|qq=}}, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|qid=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nabeta Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaga, Kumamoto|qq=}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoanabo Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmura Yokoanabo Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitoyoshi|qq=}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster|en|Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster|qid=Q11419962|lt=Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster|1=Yokkaichi Yokoanabo Cluster}} ({{ill|Usa (宇佐)|lt=Usa (宇佐)|lt=Usa|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|qid=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoanabo Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takio Hyakka Yokoanabo Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita, Ōita|qq=}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki, Miyazaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}) - National Historic Site == Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]] == References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokoanabos. *
